tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function shows mildly elevated creatinine, suggesting possible early renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, raising concern for possible tubular dysfunction or early diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is unexpectedly low. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Blood glucose is normal. Mild metabolic acidosis is suggested by low CO₂. Lipid profile is unremarkable except for slightly high HDL. Recommend clinical correlation and further nephrological and metabolic evaluation.

display.value: Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
